clinical_criteria: Age 13 years or older at time of assessment
HIV viral load measurement greater than 1000 copies/mL
Positive result on HIV confirmatory test
Exclusion for PrEP/HBV medications without other HIV evidence
algorithm_summary: The HIV phenotyping algorithm identifies patients aged 13 or older with HIV through inclusion criteria: (1) positive HIV confirmatory test results, (2) HIV viral load >1000 copies/mL, or (3) prescription for HIV-specific antiretroviral medications. Exclusion criteria remove patients under 13 years old and those prescribed only PrEP/HBV medications without other HIV evidence. Controls are patients who don't meet case criteria or are excluded from cases. The algorithm uses extensive LOINC codes for HIV confirmatory tests and viral load measurements, plus medication lists for HIV treatment vs PrEP/HBV use.
